title: 有翼之暗
summary:
The narrator confronts a silent figure, revealing that the housekeeper, Nishimura, is actually Lady Hinata, who is ninety years old. The narrator explains that the real Nishimura was placed in a coffin two years ago, allowing Hinata to assume her identity. Hinata used heavy makeup, long bangs, and her resemblance to her daughter, Tsukiyomi, to maintain this deception. Hinata had kept Tsukiyomi locked in the basement for over twenty years and orchestrated a dramatic replacement involving her daughter.

The narrator details Hinata's elaborate manipulations to frame Mistuho for the crimes. Hinata utilized a scheme involving the "Country of Origin" book series, knowing that a non-Japanese person like Mistuho would not have known that the original title of a specific book had been changed to "The Gate of Life and Death" due to the Pacific War. Hinata also poisoned Oyama with arsenic while disguised as Nishimura, and later manipulated Mitsutaka into opening Oyama's coffin to view a decapitated corpse, thereby preventing anyone from suspecting Oyama was still alive.

Furthermore, Hinata manipulated the detective Wakizaki by forging a letter from Aoi to prompt his investigation. She planted false clues, such as a foreign LP record and citron pips, to guide Wakizaki toward the false conclusion that Fujiko was the murderer. Finally, the narrator reveals that the "locked-room mystery" of the "Hell Gate" was an illusion; Hinata simply used a spare key kept in Yuki's room. The perceived impossibility of the crime was merely a result of the investigators overcomplicating a simple act of theft and murder.
